title: HAMR Energy Secures $10M for SAF and Green Methanol Projects from Qantas and Airbus
content: HAMR Energy has successfully closed a $10 million Series A funding round backed by Qantas, Airbus, and thyssenkrupp Uhde. The capital will accelerate the development of Low Carbon Liquid Fuels (LCLF) projects in Australia, specifically converting plantation forestry residues into green methanol and Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). The companyâ€™s flagship Portland Renewable Fuels project aims to produce 300,000 tonnes of low-carbon methanol annually. Furthermore, HAMR is developing Australiaâ€™s first major methanol-to-jet facility, designed to produce over 135 million litres of SAF per year using Honeywellâ€™s UOP eFiningâ„¢ technology. Supported by feedstock partner OneFortyOne, these initiatives target the decarbonization of aviation and shipping while boosting domestic fuel security. The projects are projected to create hundreds of construction jobs and 130 long-term operational roles, positioning Australia as a key player in the growing cleaner fuel market.
author: Analysts Account
